data.title: Palo Alto Networks sees bullish views at BTIG
data.description:
BTIG increased Palo Alto Networks' (PANW) price target to $380 from $333 while reiterating the stock as its top pick with a Buy rating.

Shares of the cybersecurity solutions provider rose about 4% on Wednesday.

"Our independent fieldwork on PANW the last six months has been consistently improving. And our early work into CQ’26 earnings points towards continued momentum. At a high level, PANW’s breadth across network security, cloud, endpoint, SIEM [Security Information and Event Management], observability, and identity gives it the broadest platform in cybersecurity. And our field checks indicate that platform initiatives are translating into larger deal sizes and stronger cross-sell opportunities," said analysts led by Gray Powell.

The analysts think this is showing up in results as the company is dominating its core network security target market and is substantially outgrowing peers in the Secure Access Service Edge, or SASE, space. In addition, the analysts said that Palo Alto is aggressively making inroads into the $8.6B SIEM market with XSIAM and having better than expected early success with Chronosphere. Both of these products help the company better unify its product strategy and build a data moat, according to the company.

"Lastly, we see sizable expansion opportunities in identity with CyberArk and have been picking up positive early data points on Prisma AIRS/AI Security. All in, the momentum within PANW’s installed base and expansion into higher growth markets gives us greater confidence in the company’s ability to sustain mid-teens growth over the next few years," said Powell and his team.

data.title: iPhone purchase intent increases in US, Europe but not due to Apple Intelligence: survey
data.description:
Consumers in Europe and the U.S. demonstrated an increased intent to purchase an Apple (AAPL) iPhone at some point in the next 12 months, according to a survey by UBS.

However, Apple Intelligence, which many financial analysts believed would drive up demand, is not playing a role in the increased interest.

Apple shares edged up 2% by noon trading on Wednesday.

"12-month global iPhone purchase intent in the US rose approximately 300 bps YoY to about 20%, as China fell 100 bps to 15%," said UBS analysts, led by David Vogt, in an investor report. 

The U.K. increased by 600 bps to 18%, Germany rose by 400 bps to 14%, and Japan inched up 100 bps to 13%. 

"Apple Intelligence is not driving upgrade activity as total respondents indicated that they would upgrade sooner for Apple Intelligence features declined to 24%, while those noting no impact on their purchasing decision rose to 31%," Vogt added.

The survey also found that the average age of iPhones in the user base declined to 22.9 months, but that remained near the survey's all-time high of 24.1 months. The all-time high occurred during the second quarter of 2025.

What's more, Apple is expected to reveal a new foldable phone during its annual September launch event. While the survey did not indicate significant interest in foldable smartphones in general, it did reveal meaningful consumer interest in an Apple foldable smartphone.

"Yes, but we believe a foldable iPhone will be more material in driving consumer demand than Apple's recently announced AI features," Vogt noted. "We estimate iPhone sell-in of 261.6M units (+15.7% YoY) in FY26 due to stronger-than-expected demand for the iPhone 17 and a degree of pull-in from anticipated price increases. While we estimate that incremental foldable units could be as high as 5M units (~2% upside to UBSe), our forecasts do not factor in potential demand destruction from expected iPhone price increases. As overall interest in upgrading for Apple Intelligence was relatively muted, we believe that a foldable iPhone would be a more compelling reason for consumers to upgrade, considering its favorability spread over a foldable smartphone."

data.title: Ford leads Bank of America's top 10 U.S. ideas in Quant rankings
data.description:
Bank of America has unveiled its top 10 U.S. ideas for the third quarter of 2026, highlighting companies the firm believes are well positioned across a range of sectors, with Ford Motor (F) emerging as the highest-rated stock based on Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings.

Ford earned a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.87, the highest among the bank's top picks, followed by Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX), which received a Buy rating of 4.12.

Bank of America's latest list spans financials, technology, consumer staples, pharmaceuticals and industrials. Other names include Visa (V), Snowflake (SNOW), Walmart (WMT), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), IBM (IBM), Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS), Spotify Technology (SPOT) and Lennar (LEN). These stocks carry Quant Ratings ranging from 3.45 to 2.62, placing them in the Hold category.

The bank's Q3 recommendations reflect its preference for companies with durable earnings growth, attractive industry positioning and exposure to long-term investment themes despite an uncertain macroeconomic backdrop.

Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings evaluate stocks across five core factors, valuation, growth, profitability, earnings revisions and price momentum. The system assigns scores on a scale of 1 to 5, with ratings above 3.5 considered bullish and those below 2.5 viewed as bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. Ford Motor Company (F), Quant Rating: 4.87

  2. Knight-Swift Transportation Holdings Inc. (KNX), Quant Rating: 4.12

  3. Visa Inc. (V), Quant Rating: 3.45

  4. Snowflake Inc. (SNOW), Quant Rating: 3.40

  5. Walmart Inc. (WMT), Quant Rating: 3.39

  6. JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Quant Rating: 3.33

  7. International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), Quant Rating: 3.30

  8. Ionis Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (IONS), Quant Rating: 3.14

  9. Spotify Technology S.A. (SPOT), Quant Rating: 2.73

  10. Lennar Corporation (LEN), Quant Rating: 2.62

data.title: Medicare's GLP-1 Bridge and what's next for biotech
data.description:
Investors Should Know: Medicare's new GLP-1 Bridge program is expanding access to weight-loss drugs for seniors. The move further opens the market for these popular drugs, just as other players develop their own competing products. Who is best positioned in the biotech space for the next round of development for weight loss drugs and beyond?

Background

GLP-1 drugs, a class of medications originally developed for diabetes, have become the fastest-growing category in pharmaceuticals, driven by their effectiveness in treating obesity. Until recently, Medicare did not cover GLP-1s for weight loss, leaving millions of seniors without affordable access.

The Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program changes that. This temporary Medicare demonstration program allows certain Medicare beneficiaries to receive covered GLP-1 medications for weight management for a $50 monthly copayment. The program runs through December 31, 2027.

The change in Medicare policy represents another broadening of the market for these drugs, although its future after 2027 remains up in the air. At the same time, competition in the space continues to heat up, as other companies seek to develop rival medications.

Meanwhile, the overall biotech space continues to innovate, with a wide variety of larger and smaller firms seeking to develop "the next big thing." In a capital-intense, make-or-break industry, tools like Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings can help sort a crowded field of options.

Stocks to Watch

The two companies that currently dominate the GLP-1 market are Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), holding roughly 60% and 40% market share, respectively. A broader set of competitors — including AstraZeneca (AZN), Pfizer (PFE), and Amgen (AMGN) — are developing their own GLP-1 drugs, according to a program overview. Structure Therapeutics is also among the pipeline entrants in the space.

Payer behavior is adding another layer of complexity. CVS Health (CVS) and UnitedHealth (UNH) declined to participate in a long-term payment model for the program. Separately, Cigna (CI) reportedly plans to end GLP-1 weight-loss drug coverage for its own employees effective July 1, though the company stated this does not affect diabetes GLP-1 coverage or plans outside its employee benefit program.

Key Takeaways

  • The Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program extends through 2027 and is expected to accelerate prescription volume growth as out-of-pocket costs fall for seniors.
  • Analysts project the program could add over $1 billion in annual revenue for each of the two leading GLP-1 drugmakers, with market share between them expected to remain relatively stable.
  • Oral GLP-1 formulations are gaining attention as a key adoption driver, with seniors seen as more likely to prefer pills over injections.
  • The competitive landscape is broadening, with multiple large pharmaceutical companies developing GLP-1 drugs beyond the current market leaders.
  • While GLP-1 development has gotten significant attention lately, it also represents an increasingly crowded market that could be reaching maturity. As such, investors should be aware of other players in the broader biotech space.

News Roundup

Recent developments across the GLP-1 space show how the Medicare Bridge program is reshaping access, competitive dynamics, and adjacent business models. The items below span the dominant drugmakers, payer decisions, and companies building services around GLP-1 adoption.

  • Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO) — The Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program is expected to add over $1 billion in annual revenue for each company, with their combined market share split of roughly 60/40 expected to hold steady as access expands to Medicare beneficiaries.
  • Cigna (CI) — The insurer reportedly plans to end GLP-1 obesity drug coverage for its own employees effective July 1. The company stated the change does not affect diabetes GLP-1 coverage and does not apply to plans outside its employee benefit program.
  • CVS Health (CVS) and UnitedHealth (UNH) — Both insurers declined to participate in a long-term payment model for the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program. 
  • Weight Watchers Med+ (WW) — The company issued a press release describing how it has established access to GLP-1s through the Medicare Bridge program and outlined its integrated GLP-1 Success Program and related member engagement offerings.
  • DarioHealth (DRIO) — The company announced a collaboration with Beluga Health to deliver a digitally enabled care model covering hypertension, diabetes, obesity, behavioral health, and musculoskeletal care. The partnership targets health plan customers and supports entry into Medicaid, health systems, and employer markets.

Biotech and Healthcare Innovation in Focus

The screener below identifies stocks aligned with the biotech and healthcare innovation theme that carry a Quant Rating of Buy or Strong Buy, with companies ordered by how closely they align with the biotech and healthcare innovation theme.

Other names in the screener include 4D Molecular Therapeutics (FDMT) at 4.49, Monte Rosa Therapeutics (GLUE) and Nurix Therapeutics (NRIX) both at 4.44, Innate Pharma (IPHA) at 3.79, Precision BioSciences (DTIL) at 3.73, Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) at 3.59, and Codexis (CDXS) at 3.54.

The broader healthcare screener spans multiple sub-sectors and shows ten companies with Quant Ratings between 4.91 and 4.96. Q32 Bio (QTTB) leads at 4.96, followed by Centene Corporation (CNC) at 4.95 in managed care. Health care equipment is represented by Butterfly Network (BFLY) at 4.94. Pharmaceuticals names include Liquidia Corporation (LQDA) and BioAge Labs (BIOA), both at 4.94 and 4.92 respectively. BrightSpring Health Services (BTSG) represents health care services at 4.92. Biotechnology names rounding out the list include Cue Biopharma (CUE), InflaRx (IFRX), Rallybio Corporation (RLYB), and Tango Therapeutics (TNGX), all rated between 4.91 and 4.92.

data.title: Norsk Hydro to partially restart production at Slovak aluminum smelter
data.description:
Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) said Wednesday its Slovalco aluminum joint venture reached an agreement with the Slovak government to partially restart production after a four-year shutdown, in a deal that includes a new long-term power supply contract.

The agreement would pave the way for the restart of 75K metric tons/year of smelting capacity, with production expected to resume in this year's Q4, Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) said, adding that restoring the remaining 100K tons of capacity would depend on conditions beyond 2030 and additional power contracts.

The resumption of primary aluminum production at the plant would be a boost for the European market, which has experienced a supply shortage of metal due to the closure of the Mozal smelter in Mozambique, the European Union's new carbon tax, and the Middle East war.

Slovalco, owned 55.3% by Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) and 44.7% by Penta Investments Group, was forced to cease output of primary aluminum in September 2022 as high power prices left the joint venture facing financial losses.

data.title: GM sees Q2 gains in U.S. truck and EV segments despite lower quarterly sales
data.description:
General Motors (GM) on Wednesday said its U.S. market share increased in key truck and electric vehicle segments in the second quarter, even as total sales declined on softer EV demand and inventory constraints.

The automaker reported US second quarter sales of 714,896 vehicles, down 4.2% from a year earlier.

GM (GM) said its full-size pickup truck segment share rose to about 42%, up 0.5 percentage points year-to-date, while light-duty truck sales increased 4% in the quarter and electric vehicle market share rose to a midpoint of about 13.75% year-to-date, up about 1 percentage point. 

The Detroit-based company said industry-wide U.S. light-vehicle sales are expected to run at a seasonally adjusted annual rate in the mid-16 million unit range for the full year.

GM (GM) said quarter-end inventory stood at about 551,000 vehicles, or roughly 55 days of supply, including in-transit units. Average transaction prices exceeded $52,400 in the quarter, reflecting strength in trucks, SUVs, and luxury models, alongside six entry-level vehicles priced at about $30,000 or less.

By brand, Chevrolet posted strong SUV sales, led by gains in its Trailblazer and Traverse models, while Corvette sales rose. Buick reported a record quarter for its Envista model and its strongest Enclave performance since 2021.

Cadillac said it posted its strongest-ever second-quarter EV sales, driven by OPTIQ and VISTIQ, and reported record performance for its V-Series lineup.

GM said GMC Sierra and Terrain posted retail gains, with Terrain and Canyon sales rising in the first half.

Shares of the automaker were down about 1.2% on Wednesday.

data.title: Guardant Health rises following UnitedHealth coverage of Shield test
data.description:
• Guardant Health (GH) is up ~7% in Wednesday morning trading after UnitedHealth Group (UNH) said it would cover the company's blood-based cancer screening test, Guardant Shield, starting Aug. 1.
  • The test is used to screen for colorectal cancer.
  • Citi analyst Patrick Donnelly said the move is "another positive catalyst for Shield as this expansion of the covered patient opportunity unlocks a new cohort of volumes and should drive significant ASP benefits."

data.title: Rio Tinto, Mongolia government reach new financial terms for Oyu Tolgoi mine project
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) said late Tuesday it reached new financial terms with the Mongolian government for the $18B Oyu Tolgoi copper mining project, following months of negotiation over the country's single biggest mining project and foreign investment.

The company said it agreed to cut its management fees for the project in the Gobi Desert by 50% and reduce the interest rate on its multibillion-dollar loan to Mongolia by 2.5 percentage points.

Rio (RIO) Chair Dominic Barton and head of copper Katie Jackson met Mongolian Prime MinisterUchral Nyam-Osor on Tuesday in Ulan Bator to sign the deal.

The agreement showed Mongolia could "protect our financial sovereignty while maintaining ahighly lucrative environment for global capital," the prime minister said, and Jackson said the deal shows the company's "ongoing commitment to the long-term success of Oyu Tolgoi" and that the reduced interest rate reflects the lower risk as the project matures.

Mongolian officials had called Oyu Tolgoi's earlier investment terms "unfair," and the new agreement comes as copper prices are near record highs, potentially boosting future payouts from the mine, which is expected to eventually produce ~500K metric tons/year of copper.

data.title: Hyperscaler slump is a buying opportunity ahead of earnings - Goldman
data.description:
Goldman Sachs is advising investors to capitalize on the recent tech sell-off by buying the dip in US hyperscalers (AMZN) (MSFT) (GOOG) (GOOGL) (META) (ORCL) as the upcoming earnings season approaches.

Goldman's hyperscaler index has suffered a sharp 17% drawdown since peaking on June 1, dropping from a high of 122.89 down to 102.46.

Despite this recent price correction, analysts highlight a growing divergence: underlying earnings for these companies have continued to climb steadily. The recent stock price decline is largely attributed to investor fatigue and hesitation, as the market temporarily refrains from rewarding massive capital expenditures.

Consequently, hyperscaler valuations have compressed significantly, with the basket now trading at lows similar to earlier "liberation day" bottoms and the peak geopolitical risk lows seen earlier this year.

The investment bank argues that this pullback presents a highly compelling entry point. The primary catalyst for a rebound lies in upcoming earnings calls. If these tech giants can successfully demonstrate tangible revenue growth resulting from their heavy AI-related capex investments, analysts believe the group is well-positioned for a swift recovery that could quickly erase the recent retreat in shares.
